fills.note: Jonah, not Ruth — there is no Jonah BCD ref in my inputs, so this is a best-guess parallel to ruth_pilot_BCD_v0_3. The canonical ref is the reviewer's call.

fills.value_json: {"_note":"Three moment-level register shifts inside reported speech per MM Section 1; no scene-level overrides; no NARRATIVE_FRAMING shift. Verses are English numbering.","scene_level":null,"moment_level":[{"verse":"1:6","override_value":"ELDER_AUTHORITY"},{"verse":"1:7","override_value":"CONSULTATIVE"},{"verse":"1:11","override_value":"CONSULTATIVE"},{"verse":"1:14","override_value":"RELIGIOUS_WORSHIP"}]}
fills.note: MM Section 1 marks the captain's charge (v.6) as ELDER_AUTHORITY, the sailors' deliberations (v.7, v.11) as CONSULTATIVE, and the crew's prayer (v.14) as RELIGIOUS_WORSHIP.
The approved arc_element list is Ruth-specific; Jonah's storm narrative has no fits, so these are mints tracking the prose movement.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "YHWH hurls a great wind at the sea, and the storm that follows hunts one man" — the storm as divine pursuit opens the arc.
"The sailors do everything — pray, jettison, cast lots, interrogate, row."
"the lot finds Jonah" — the runaway is exposed.
"his own mouth confesses the God he is fleeing."
"his only counsel is 'hurl me in.'"
"the men dig their oars in to get back to the dry land — and they cannot."
"Hurled at last into the sea."
"the storm stops dead" / "the sea stands still from its raging."
"the pagan crew worships YHWH" — fear, sacrifice, vows.
"YHWH appoints a great fish to swallow the man" — rescue in the shape of judgment closes the arc.

fills.note: Pagan seamen each with his own god (story-world); the sea (physical-location); lot-casting custom (institutional); 'fear YHWH' idiom and innocent-blood guilt (audience-knowledge); direct continuation of J01 (prior-pericope carry-forward); Jonah's paid flight (prior-action); the God he flees acting through wind/lot/fish (divine).
IRONIC is a mint — the MM makes irony a load-bearing tone feature explicitly.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the irony rides on top of it ... the prophet of YHWH is fast asleep below while every pagan aboard is praying" — irony is named as the passage's pervasive tone, with no approved token for it.

fills.note: Alarm builds in waves (RISES), fast crowded verbs (BRISK), sudden flat calm at v.15 (SETTLES), three days of silence (HOLDS).

Carries J01's flight into consequence (ADVANCES); puts the fled command back in Jonah's ears (REACTIVATES); sets up the psalm J03 (STAGES); withholds the rescue announcement and the silence inside the fish (WITHHOLDS).
YHWH as storm-sender; reusing the approved DIVINE_AGENT role.
No marked referential form for YHWH here.
The sailors as a collective working crew; mint.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the sailors" / "the men" — the ship's working crew, with no approved maritime collective role in the list.

fills.note: MM marks the naming here as 'the sailors' (hammalachim), set up to contrast with the later shift to 'the men.'
The chief of the sailors who orders Jonah to pray; mint.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the captain ... rab hachovel, the chief of the sailors" — master of the ship; no approved equivalent.

fills.note: MM gives the marked referential form 'the chief of the sailors' (rab hachovel).
Jonah the runaway, asleep below; mint.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the runaway ... the prophet fleeing YHWH" — Jonah's scene function throughout the pericope; no approved fit.

fills.note: MM flags it: 'the captain addresses him only as sleeper — no name, no office.'
Mint; approved scene_kind list is Ruth-specific.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "The storm hits, and the prophet sleeps" — YHWH hurls the wind, the storm rises, the crew responds while Jonah sleeps.

fills.note: Same crew casting lots and questioning; mint (re-declared).

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the men" — the ship's working crew, continued from S1.

fills.note: MM marks the shift: 'from v.10 the narrator calls them the men — no longer the sailors.'
Jonah, the exposed/confessing runaway; mint (re-declared).

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the one the lot falls on; the questioned man; the confessor" — still the runaway, now exposed.

fills.note: MM flags it: 'he names himself a Hebrew — his people, not his calling.'
YHWH confessed as maker of sea and dry land; approved role.
The divine descriptors (God of heaven, maker of sea and dry land) are confession content, carried in the proposition slots, not a marked narrator naming.
Mint.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "The lot finds him, and he confesses" — the lot exposes Jonah and his own mouth confesses YHWH.

fills.note: Crew asking and rowing; mint (re-declared).

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the men ... rowing hard for shore to spare him" — continued crew.

fills.note: No marked form flagged in this scene.
Jonah counseling his own hurling; mint (re-declared).

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the one who says: lift me and hurl me into the sea" — the runaway who owns the storm.

fills.note: No marked form flagged here.
Mint.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "'Hurl me in' — and they row instead" — the crew weighs what to do as the sea rises, trying to spare him before throwing him.

fills.note: Crew praying, hurling, worshiping; mint (re-declared).

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the ones who pray, hurl Jonah in, and then fear and worship YHWH".

fills.note: No marked form for the crew flagged here.
YHWH prayed to, feared, worshiped; approved role.
No marked narrator naming; 'cries to him by name' is relationship, not a marked form.
Jonah lifted and hurled in; mint (re-declared).

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the man lifted and hurled into the sea" — 'this man' of the crew's prayer.

fills.note: MM flags it: 'in the prayer he is this man, a soul that might be charged as innocent blood.'
Mint.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "Overboard: the sea stands still, and the men worship" — the prayer, the hurling, the calm, and the crew's worship in one scene.

fills.note: YHWH appoints the fish; approved role.
No marked form here.
The great fish doing YHWH's errand; mint.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the appointed swallower ... YHWH's creature, doing YHWH's errand without argument" — no approved role for an appointed non-human agent.

fills.note: 'great fish' is descriptive, not a map-flagged marked naming.
Jonah swallowed, at last praying; mint (re-declared).

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the swallowed man; at last, the one who prays" — the runaway carried alive.

fills.note: No marked form flagged here.
Mint.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "The fish: swallowed, three days, and a prayer begins" — YHWH appoints the fish, it swallows Jonah, and the prayer begins.

fills.note: Mint; the storm-hurling refrain (FIG_0199) has no approved proposition_kind.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "hurling ... YHWH hurled a great wind at the sea" — the hetil/hurl verb that recurs four times in the pericope.

fills.note: Mint.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "a great storm rose on the sea, and the ship was about to break apart."

fills.note: Mint; the fear-escalation refrain (FIG_0200).

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the sailors are afraid; each cries to his own god" — first step of the fear escalation.

fills.note: The crying to own gods is reported without quoted content, so no speech_act component is given.
Mint (re-declared); second throw of the hurling refrain.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "they hurl the cargo into the sea to lighten the ship" — the second of four hurls.

fills.note: Mint; the descent refrain (FIG_0198).

vocabulary_additions.justification: "Jonah has gone down into the far hold, lain down, and fallen fast asleep" — the yarad descent that runs through J01-J02.

fills.note: Concurrent ('meanwhile') with the crew's actions, so no caused_by from the storm chain.
Mint.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the captain comes to him" and rebukes the sleeper — the captain's approach with a protest question.

fills.note: 'What are you doing, sleeper?' read as a rebuke/protest rather than information-seeking.
The captain finds him because he had gone down to sleep.
Reusing approved INSTRUCTION; the captain's command (FIG_0201, echo of 1:2).
Commands encoded per the action-idiom ruling: DIRECTED + commanded_step, not imperatives on the action axis.
Mint on proposition_kind axis (PROPOSED exists on the action axis but not proposition_kind).

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the men say to one another: come, let us cast lots" — a cohortative proposal among equals (the CONSULTATIVE register shift at v.7).

fills.note: Mint.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "they cast, and the lot falls on Jonah" — the lot-casting that exposes the runaway.

fills.note: Mint.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "they press him: tell us — whose fault is this? what is your work? ..." — the rapid-fire questioning of the exposed man.

fills.note: The five questions preserved as a listing; the MM's significant absence (Jonah never answers 'what is your work?') is carried at the scene level.
Reusing approved DECLARED for Jonah's answer 'I am a Hebrew.'
identity_kind preserves the MM's point: he gives his people, not his prophetic calling.
Mint; CB_0053 fear-of-YHWH active here.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "I fear YHWH, God of heaven, who made the sea and the dry land" — Jonah's confession of the God he flees.

fills.note: Mint (re-declared); second step of the fear escalation (great fear).

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the men fear a great fear and say, What is this you have done!" — the escalated fear.

fills.note: paired_with P3 marks the fear-escalation rhyme (FIG_0200).
Reusing approved PERCEIVED for the men's knowing.
CB_0052 (from-before-YHWH) and FIG_0197 active — the J01 refrain sounds again.
Mint on proposition_kind axis (ASKED exists on the action axis but not proposition_kind).

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the men ask Jonah: what shall we do to you, so the sea will quiet?" — the crew's question to the exposed man (CONSULTATIVE shift at v.11).

fills.note: Mint; Jonah's directive answer (FIG_0199 hurling refrain — 'hurl me').

vocabulary_additions.justification: "he answers: lift me and hurl me into the sea" — a response to the crew's question that is itself a directive counsel.

fills.note: The 'hurl me' counsel encoded as DIRECTED steps; storm_ownership_admission preserves 'on my account.' The MM's absence — no offer to repent or turn to Nineveh — is carried at scene level.
paired_with P15 — Jonah's answer responds to the crew's question.
Mint.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the men dig their oars in to get back to the dry land — and they cannot" — the failed rescue effort.

fills.note: Reusing approved APPEAL for the crew's prayer to YHWH (RELIGIOUS_WORSHIP shift at v.14).
CB_0054 (innocent blood) active. Petitions to YHWH encoded as DIRECTS_HEARER_NOT_TO_DO (the hearer being YHWH).
Mint (re-declared); fourth throw of the hurling refrain — Jonah himself.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "they lift Jonah and hurl him into the sea" — the climactic hurl of the refrain.

fills.note: action_sequence kept as descriptive tokens rather than the action axis, since neither lift nor hurl is an approved action value.
Mint.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the sea stands still from its raging" — the sudden calm that is the storm's verdict.

fills.note: Mint; CB_0053 active, FIG_0200 third step (feared YHWH a great fear).

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the men fear YHWH with a great fear, and they offer a sacrifice ... and they vow vows" — the pagan crew's full worship as the storm's outcome.

fills.note: The trio fear/sacrifice/vow kept as worship_acts; the vow carries the VOWS speech act.
paired_with P13 completes the fear-escalation chain (FIG_0200: feared / feared a great fear / feared YHWH a great fear).
Mint; the appointing refrain that recurs across Jonah (fish, plant, worm, wind).

vocabulary_additions.justification: "YHWH appoints a great fish to swallow Jonah" — the manah/appoint verb.

fills.note: Mint.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "the fish swallows him. Jonah is in the belly of the fish three days and three nights."

fills.note: Duration referenced to the scene object TH_THREE_DAYS_AND_THREE_NIGHTS; exact form preserved.
Mint; the hinge into the psalm J03.

vocabulary_additions.justification: "Jonah prays to YHWH his God from the belly of the fish" — the prayer the prophet never prayed on deck.

fills.note: The prayer content itself begins in J03, so no quoted speech_act is carried here.
Terminal proposition of the pericope; no forward link.

No further decomposition needed beyond the components already used inside event_specific_slots: P7 (captain's two-step command), P10 (five-part interrogation listing), P16 (Jonah's two-step 'hurl me' counsel), P18 (two-part crew petition), and P21 (fear/sacrifice/vow worship_acts). The remaining propositions are single-event and left at meaning-map granularity. Flags are anchored by verse as given in the MM.

remarks: This is a Jonah pericope; the approved bounded-open lists are heavily Ruth-shaped, so the storm narrative required substantial declared mints on arc_element (10), tone_element (IRONIC), role_in_scene_being (CREW, SHIP_CAPTAIN, FUGITIVE, APPOINTED_CREATURE), scene_kind (5), and proposition_kind (HURLED, STORM_AROSE, FEARED, WENT_DOWN, APPROACHED, PROPOSED, CAST_LOTS, INTERROGATED, CONFESSED, ASKED, ANSWERED, ROWED, SEA_STILLED, WORSHIPED, APPOINTED, SWALLOWED, PRAYED). Where an approved value genuinely fit I reused it (DIVINE_AGENT; INSTRUCTION, DECLARED, PERCEIVED, APPEAL on proposition_kind; DIRECTED/VOWED on the action axis; the SPEECH_ACT closed list throughout). Re-declared values (CREW, FUGITIVE, HURLED, FEARED) appear in each fill they occur in per the strict declare-every-mint rule. book_context_ref is a best-guess Jonah analogue and needs the reviewer's canonical ref. Referential_form was marked only where the MM's own prose flags the naming (the hammalachim→ha'anashim shift, 'the chief of the sailors', 'sleeper', self-named 'a Hebrew', 'this man' in the prayer) and left null elsewhere. The English/Hebrew versification note (Eng 1:17–2:1 = Heb 2:1–2) is honored via the MM's English anchors. Significant_absence blocks were left as the deterministic skeleton carried them — they are load-bearing (Jonah's prayerlessness, the unspoken 'prophet', no word of sin, no rescue announced).
